task: Add input validation to the login() function in src/auth.js so it rejects an empty username with a clear error.

input.query: add input validation to login() in src/auth.js to reject empty username

input.learning: login() in src/auth.js validates that the normalized username is non-empty and throws Error("username is required") before calling db.check. The check runs after normalizeUsername so whitespace-only inputs (e.g. "   ") are also rejected. This is additive to the existing normalize-before-lookup convention, not a replacement.
input.title: login rejects empty username after normalization
input.evidence: src/auth.js:5 - `if (!key) throw new Error("username is required");` placed immediately after `const key = normalizeUsername(username);`

input.reason: login() in src/auth.js now both normalizes the username and rejects empty values; the replacement packet captures the fuller behavior (normalize-then-validate) so the original normalize-only claim is subsumed.
